title: Fundamental and Non-Fundamental Factors Determining Hedging Decisions: Miscellaneous Industry Companies in ASEAN, 2016–2021
author: Frisca Novia Sukmawati
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Intra-ASEAN international trade has increased firms’ exposure to currency risk, where exchange rate fluctuations significantly affect cash flow and financial performance, making hedging an essential risk management tool. This study examines the influence of fundamental and non-fundamental factors on corporate hedging decisions using a quantitative approach based on secondary financial statement data. The sample consists of 115 firms selected using the purposive sampling method and analyzed through panel data logistic regression. Results show that liquidity, leverage, and CEO gender negatively affect hedging decisions, while firm size positively affect hedging decisions size, exchange rates and managerial ownership are not significant. These findings partially contradict risk management theory, particularly regarding exchange rate exposure, as higher risk does not necessarily lead to increased hedging as a mitigation strategy.
steps_raw: Intra-ASEAN international trade has increased firms’ exposure to currency risk, where exchange rate fluctuations significantly affect cash flow and financial performance, making hedging an essential risk management tool. The sample consists of 115 firms selected using the purposive sampling method and analyzed through panel data logistic regression. Results show that liquidity, leverage, and CEO gender negatively affect hedging decisions, while firm size positively affect hedging decisions size, exchange rates and managerial ownership are not significant. These findings partially contradict risk management theory, particularly regarding exchange rate exposure, as higher risk does not necessarily lead to increased hedging as a mitigation strategy.
keywords: hedging, risk management, exchange rate, leverage, liquidity, firm size, CEO gender, managerial ownership.
